title: Pterolepis ladanoides
taxonomy: Pterolepis ladanoides is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The classification of Pterolepis ladanoides typically falls within the genus Pterolepis, which is a part of the family Ericaceae. The family Ericaceae is further categorized under the order Ericales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Pterolepis ladanoides is a flowering plant species, commonly known as a type of shrub or small tree, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterolepis ladanoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pterolepis ladanoides is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to treat coughs, sore throats, and skin infections.
